modules.provides: User authentication and account access: sign-up and sign-in with email or phone one-time codes, passwords, social (OAuth) accounts, corporate SSO (SAML/OIDC), magic links, QR hand-off and passkeys; guest (anonymous) access; two-factor authentication and step-up verification; session and device management with a security audit trail.

curated.summary: Visitors get a temporary anonymous account (e.g. to browse or fill a cart) that can later be upgraded to a full account; guest data merges on upgrade.
Users can protect their account with time-based one-time codes (Google Authenticator etc.); also the factor behind step-up checks on sensitive actions.
curated.business_label: Two-factor authentication with an authenticator app

curated.summary: New users can create an account by confirming their phone number with a one-time SMS code.
New users can create an account by confirming their email with a one-time code.
New users can create an account through Google, GitHub, Apple and other configured OAuth providers.
Employees signing in through their company identity provider (SAML/OIDC) get an account provisioned on first login.
curated.business_label: Automatic accounts for corporate SSO users

curated.summary: New users can register with a login and password. Off by default — passwordless methods are the house default.
Existing users sign in by receiving a one-time SMS code.
Existing users sign in by receiving a one-time code by email.
Existing users sign in through the configured OAuth providers.
Users of onboarded organizations sign in through their company identity provider (SAML/OIDC), configured per organization in the database.
Existing users sign in with login and password (plus change/reset flows). Off by default.
A signed-in device shows a QR code; scanning it signs the user in on the new device.
curated.business_label: Sign-in by QR code from another device

curated.summary: Users sign in with platform passkeys (Face ID, fingerprint, hardware security keys) via WebAuthn; also usable as a second factor.
Users sign in by clicking a short-lived link sent to their email — no code entry.
curated.business_label: Sign-in via a one-time email link

curated.summary: Controls whether the email one-time-code method renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
curated.business_label: Where the email sign-in method appears

curated.summary: Controls whether the phone one-time-code method renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
curated.business_label: Where the phone sign-in method appears

curated.summary: Controls whether password sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
curated.business_label: Where the password sign-in method appears

curated.summary: Controls whether the one-time email-link method renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
curated.business_label: Where the email-link sign-in method appears

curated.summary: Controls whether corporate SSO renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
Controls whether social (OAuth) sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
Controls whether QR hand-off sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
Controls whether passkey sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

gates.behavior: When on, a successful OAuth login with a TOTP-enrolled account returns a TOTP challenge instead of tokens.

curated.summary: Require a two-factor code even after a social (OAuth) login. Off by default — the provider already authenticated the user.

gates.behavior: When on, a successful password login with a TOTP-enrolled account returns a TOTP challenge instead of tokens.

curated.summary: Require a two-factor code after password login for TOTP-enrolled accounts. On by default — a password alone is phishable.

extension_points.name: OAUTH_PROVIDER_CLASSES
extension_points.summary: Register additional OAuth providers (subclass stapel_core.oauth.OAuthProvider in the host app, append the dotted path) — no fork.

extension_points.name: REREGISTRATION_MODEL
extension_points.summary: Swap the model that stores re-registration hashes; resolved lazily, stapel-gdpr is not a hard dependency.

extension_points.name: AUTH_USER_MODEL
extension_points.summary: Standard Django user swap — subclass stapel-core's AbstractStapelUser; this module only ever uses get_user_model().

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every user-facing view declares *_request/_response_serializer_class seams (SerializerSeamsMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, mount via the URL factories.

extension_points.name: STAPEL_VERIFICATION.EXTRA_FACTORS
extension_points.summary: Add step-up verification factors to the stapel-core registry alongside the built-ins this module registers (otp_email, otp_phone, totp, passkey).

extension_points.name: url_factories
extension_points.summary: Compose your own URLconf from the get_*_urls() factories so disabled features 404 (include('stapel_auth.urls') mounts everything with per-request gating).

requires.reason: comm bus (events/functions), JWT provider, verification mechanism, OAuth provider registry
default REREGISTRATION_MODEL target (lazy dotted path); co-mounted under /auth/api/ in the contract harness
delivers the OTP / magic-link / security emails and SMS this module requests via notification.requested

flows.title: Password login (+ optional TOTP)
flows.description: The user signs in with a login (email/username) and password. The endpoint is enabled by the AUTH_PASSWORD_LOGIN setting. Failed attempts lead to progressive lockout (423 with retry_after). If the user has TOTP enabled and the PASSWORD_LOGIN_STEP_UP setting is active (default: yes), TOTP_REQUIRED with a challenge_token is returned instead of tokens — the session is issued only after the authenticator code is verified.

steps.note: The user enters their login and password on the login form
Verify the password; 423 when locked out; with TOTP enabled and PASSWORD_LOGIN_STEP_UP — a TOTP_REQUIRED response with a challenge_token
Optional step (only on TOTP_REQUIRED): exchange the challenge_token and the authenticator code for a JWT session

flows.title: Passwordless login (email OTP)
flows.description: An anonymous user receives a one-time code by email and exchanges it for a JWT session (cookies + a token pair in the response body). Requesting the code again is rate-limited (30 seconds between sends; 429/422 when exceeded); after a series of wrong codes the address is temporarily locked. If the address was not registered, the first successful login creates a new user (status=REGISTERED instead of LOGGED_IN).

steps.note: The user enters their email on the login form
Request a one-time code by email; 429 on rate limit, 422 when the address is locked
Exchange the code for a JWT session; a wrong code decrements the attempt counter
Emitted on first login — the profile and workspace are created by subscribers

flows.title: Step-up verification on a protected endpoint (reference flow)
flows.description: THE reference flow of the step-up verification contract (stapel_core.verification, see flows-and-verification.md §2) — clients of any service implement it once and reuse it for every endpoint protected by @requires_verification. The cycle: the protected endpoint responds 403 with a structured verification envelope (challenge_id, scope, factors, expires_at) → the client reads the challenge, picks an available factor (factors are interchangeable: otp_email, otp_phone, totp, passkey all close one challenge), initiates it and completes the check → repeats the original request. The grant is stored server-side (cache, user+scope key, TTL=max_age); stateless clients may instead send the X-Verification-Token header from the completion response. After MAX_ATTEMPTS wrong attempts the challenge burns out (423) — call the original endpoint again for a new challenge.

steps.note: The client calls the protected endpoint and receives 403 with a verification envelope: challenge_id, scope, factors, expires_at
Read the challenge: the scope and the factors filtered down to those actually available to the user; 404 for a foreign/expired challenge
Initiate the chosen factor: send a code (otp_email/otp_phone) or get WebAuthn options (passkey); totp needs no initiation
Complete the challenge with the factor proof; success = {verified, verification_token} + a server-side grant; 400 on a wrong code, 423 when the challenge burned out from brute force
Repeat the original request — the grant is already on the server; a stateless client sends the X-Verification-Token from the completion response

Optional: view your step-up preferences — one {scope, enabled} row per scope the user has touched (enabled=false disables a default_on scope, enabled=true enables an opt_in scope; strict endpoints ignore the preferences)

Optional: change a {scope, enabled} preference. INVARIANT: disabling (enabled=false) is itself protected by @requires_verification(scope=verification.settings, level=default_on) — without a fresh grant a 403 with a verification envelope is returned; enabling requires no step-up confirmation. Both writes reset the policy cache in core

modules.provides: Payments and billing: per-user credit wallets with an immutable transaction ledger, one-off credit packages and recurring subscription plans, Stripe-backed checkout and self-service customer portal, idempotent webhooks and a service-to-service debit endpoint.

curated.summary: Which payment backend handles checkout, subscriptions and webhooks. Stripe out of the box; any other processor plugs in as a host-project provider class.

extension_points.name: CREDIT_PACKAGES
extension_points.summary: Replace the one-off credit package catalog from settings (list of dicts or CreditPackage instances) — re-read lazily on every access, no fork.

extension_points.name: PLANS
extension_points.summary: Replace the subscription plan catalog from settings (list of dicts or PlanCatalogEntry instances) — re-read lazily on every access, no fork.

extension_points.name: PAYMENT_PROVIDER
extension_points.summary: Swap the payment backend: implement the PaymentProvider ABC (checkout, portal, cancel, webhook verify) in the host project and point the setting at it.

extension_points.name: AUTH_USER_MODEL
extension_points.summary: Standard Django user swap — wallet/subscription rows bind to settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL; the module never references a concrete user class.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every billing view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (payment.completed / subscription.changed emits, user.deleted consumer), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry, AppSettings config layer

issues the authenticated user sessions the wallet/checkout/subscription endpoints require and the user.deleted event this module consumes; the model binding itself is the standard AUTH_USER_MODEL swap

modules.provides: Calendar and scheduling: events with participants and RSVP, RFC 5545 recurring series (virtual expansion, on-demand materialization, per-instant cancellation and reschedule), availability windows with free/busy and bookable slot computation, ICS export, and event-driven reminders.

gates.behavior: participants: event read access is limited to its invitees; scope: events are visible to the whole resolved scope (workspace/org/tenant).

curated.summary: Controls the read surface of every event. "participants" (default) shows an event only to the people invited to it — the private-calendar model. "scope" makes events visible to everyone in the same workspace/org/tenant (as resolved by the scope provider) — a shared team calendar. Values: participants, scope. It changes what listing, calendar and detail endpoints return, not which endpoints exist.

extension_points.name: REMINDER_POLICY
extension_points.summary: Swap the reminder policy (when and what to remind) — the default emits one reminder_due event per configured offset; subclass to change cadence or channel decisions without forking.

extension_points.name: SCOPE_PROVIDER
extension_points.summary: Swap how the opaque scope_key (workspace/org/tenant) is resolved from the request and enforced on querysets — the default is a single global scope; a host may return the active workspace_id.

extension_points.name: PRESETS
extension_points.summary: Add or override recurrence presets (dateutil rrule kwargs) merged over the built-ins (none/daily/weekdays/weekly/biweekly/monthly/custom); None removes a built-in.

extension_points.name: calendar.occurrence.materialized
extension_points.summary: The resource hook: when a recurring occurrence is materialized the engine emits this event (plus a Django signal) — the app layer subscribes to attach its resource (e.g. a video room) without any coupling in the engine.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (occurrence.materialized / reminder_due emits, calendar.free_busy Function), JWT authentication, AppSettings config layer
every endpoint requires an authenticated user (IsAuthenticated); stapel-auth is the shelf's session issuer — any stapel-core-compatible JWT issuer satisfies the check
delivers the calendar.event.reminder_due events the default reminder policy emits; reminders degrade to no-ops without a subscriber

config_md: | VISIBILITY | env | config axis: `participants` (event visible only to invitees) or `scope` (visible to the whole SCOPE_PROVIDER scope). Unknown value degrades to `participants`. | no | participants |
| DEFAULT_EXPANSION_HORIZON_DAYS | env | days ahead to expand an unbounded recurring series for display. | no | 90 |
| MAX_EXPANSION_OCCURRENCES | env | hard safety cap on occurrences a single expansion may yield. | no | 1000 |
| REMINDER_OFFSETS | env | minutes-before-start the default policy fires `calendar.event.reminder_due`. | no | [10] |
| REMINDER_SCAN_WINDOW_SECONDS | env | granularity (seconds) of the reminder cron scan. | no | 60 |
| REMINDER_POLICY | env | dotted path to a ReminderPolicy (the reminder seam). | no | stapel_calendar.reminders.DefaultReminderPolicy |
| SCOPE_PROVIDER | env | dotted path to a ScopeProvider (resolve/filter the opaque scope_key). | no | stapel_calendar.scope.DefaultScopeProvider |
| PRESETS | env | recurrence presets merged OVER the built-ins (dateutil.rrule kwargs); None removes. | no | {} |
| DEFAULT_SLOT_MINUTES | env | default slot length (minutes) for availability computation. | no | 30 |

modules.provides: Conversations and messaging: direct (1:1, idempotent by participant pair), group and support threads on one model; text/system messages with a monotonic per-conversation seq, replies and opaque CDN attachment keys; per-participant read markers and unread counts; anchor-paginated history (canonical anchor=seq) and conversation lists; a support layer (unassigned queue, first-come assignment, open/pending/resolved with reopen); and an optional Channels consumer for realtime delivery with seq-replay resume.

gates.behavior: A create/operate request for a kind not in CHAT_KINDS is rejected (400); the support queue/assign/resolve/reopen endpoints answer 400 when "support" is not enabled.

curated.summary: The enabled subset of direct (1:1), group and support threads. Drop "support" to run a pure peer-to-peer chat with no operator queue or assignment; drop "group" to allow only 1:1 direct threads. Creating (or, for support, operating on) a kind that is not enabled is refused. Values: any non-empty subset of direct, group, support.

gates.behavior: off: a message with a non-empty attachments list is rejected (400); on: attachment keys are accepted and stored on the message.

curated.summary: When on, a message may carry a list of opaque attachment keys (the files live in the host's CDN/storage; the module stores only the keys). When off, any message with an attachment is rejected. Text is always allowed.

gates.behavior: A body longer than MAX_BODY_LENGTH characters is rejected (400) at the API boundary.

curated.summary: Hard cap, in characters, on a single text message body. A longer body is rejected before it is stored. Raise it for long-form support replies, lower it for terse chat.

extension_points.name: SCOPE_PROVIDER
extension_points.summary: Swap how the opaque scope_key (workspace/org/tenant) is resolved from the request and enforced on querysets — the default is a single global scope; a host may return the active workspace_id so conversations are partitioned per tenant.

extension_points.name: chat.message
extension_points.summary: Emitted (transactionally, via the outbox) whenever a message is appended. Realtime delivery, search indexing and notifications subscribe without any coupling in the engine.

extension_points.name: chat.support.assigned
extension_points.summary: Emitted when a support conversation is claimed by an operator — routing and operator-notification layers subscribe.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (chat.message / chat.support.assigned emits via the outbox), JWT authentication (HTTP + optional Channels), AppSettings config layer, anchor pagination
every endpoint requires an authenticated user (IsAuthenticated); stapel-auth is the shelf's session issuer — any stapel-core-compatible JWT issuer satisfies the check
messages carry opaque attachment keys only; a file/CDN module (or any host storage) owns the bytes those keys point at

config_md: | CHAT_KINDS | env | axis: enabled subset of {direct, group, support}. Dropping `support` removes the operator queue; dropping `group` leaves only 1:1. | no | [direct, group, support] |
| ATTACHMENTS | env | axis: whether messages may carry attachment keys (opaque CDN keys; no files stored). When off, an attachment-bearing message is rejected. | no | true |
| MAX_BODY_LENGTH | env | axis: hard cap on a text message body (characters). | no | 4000 |
| SCOPE_PROVIDER | env | dotted path to a ScopeProvider (resolve/filter the opaque scope_key). | no | stapel_chat.scope.DefaultScopeProvider |

modules.provides: Multi-channel user notifications: routes typed notification requests to email, SMS and push with per-user preferences and language, branded email templates, device push-token management and an in-app notification feed; delivery is logged per channel with idempotency.

curated.summary: Which service sends the emails: Resend, SMTP, Mailgun, a mock for development, or any custom provider class supplied by the host project.
Which service sends the text messages: GatewayAPI, Twilio, a mock for development, or any custom provider class supplied by the host project.
Which service delivers mobile/web push: Firebase Cloud Messaging (default), a mock for development, or any custom provider class supplied by the host project.

extension_points.name: TYPES
extension_points.summary: Register new notification types (channels + group + template) or override built-ins entirely from settings — merged over the packaged routing catalog, no fork.

extension_points.name: EMAIL_TEMPLATES
extension_points.summary: Remap any notification type to a host-project email template; merged over the packaged per-type template map.

extension_points.name: channel_providers
extension_points.summary: The EMAIL/SMS/PUSH provider settings accept any dotted path to a class with the matching send() signature — add SendGrid, Postmark, APNs etc. as host-project classes, no fork.

extension_points.name: template_overrides
extension_points.summary: Packaged email templates live under a namespaced path and follow standard Django template-loader precedence; eject_notification_templates copies them into the host project for structural edits, and LOGO_URL/BRAND_*/COMPANY_* settings rebrand every email without touching a template.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (notification.requested input, translate.resolve calls), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry, AppSettings config layer
source of the user-contact sync (email/phone projections) and the user.deleted / user.deletion_initiated events this module consumes; decoupled via the comm bus
source of the notification-preferences and language sync (profile-changed events consumed into UserNotificationSettings); decoupled via the comm bus
notification texts are localized through the translate.resolve comm Function with lazy caching; without it the built-in English defaults are used

modules.provides: User profiles and social graph: display name, avatar, locale/currency/measurement preferences, notification opt-outs, and follow/unfollow/block relationships between users, with a relationship-status lookup.

curated.summary: Controls how the profile service confirms an uploaded avatar reference actually exists on the CDN before saving it: through the internal comm bus (default) or skipped entirely (only the reference format is still validated).

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every profile/relationship view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamsMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL; keep calling super() in serializer overrides so profile.changed keeps publishing.

extension_points.name: profile_extras_relation
extension_points.summary: Extend the profile without a fork: an app-layer model with a OneToOne to profiles.Profile (or keyed by the same user_id UUID), exposed through the serializer seams — models here are deliberately not swappable.

extension_points.name: profile.changed
extension_points.summary: The primary hook for syncing derived data: emitted on every profile mutation (keyed by user_id) — subscribe with @on_action from any module or the host app, no import of this package.

requires.reason: comm bus (events/functions), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry, AppSettings config layer
consumes the user.registered / user.deleted comm events (OAuth avatar import, GDPR erasure on account deletion) that stapel-auth emits; decoupled via the comm bus, not a hard import dependency
avatar storage/existence checks (PROFILES_AVATAR_CHECK=comm calls cdn.media_exists) and OAuth avatar import (cdn.import_from_url) go through stapel_core.comm to a CDN provider
surfaces this module's registered GDPR provider (profile section export/erase) through DSR endpoints; the registration itself only needs stapel-core.gdpr

modules.provides: Recording lifecycle: audio/video upload (presigned single-PUT and multipart), storage, a configurable processing pipeline (convert, transcribe, diarize, merge) producing a unified speaker-attributed transcript, optional automatic summaries, and a watchdog that recovers stuck or abandoned recordings.

gates.behavior: When on, the merge stage calls llm.summarize on the finished transcript and stores the result; a summary failure never fails the recording.

curated.summary: When on, each completed transcript is additionally summarized (via the agent module's language model) and the summary is stored on the recording; the transcript itself is always produced.

extension_points.name: PIPELINE
extension_points.summary: The flagship seam: the processing pipeline is an ordered list of stage names run by a generic driver — reorder, subset (e.g. skip diarization) or insert stages (e.g. PII redaction) purely from settings, no fork.

extension_points.name: STAGES
extension_points.summary: Replace, remove or add stage handlers ({name: dotted-path | None}, merged over the built-ins) or register them at runtime via register_stage().

extension_points.name: PIPELINE_RESOLVER
extension_points.summary: Source the stage list at runtime per recording — point it at a DB or per-workspace definition so operators can edit pipelines in a UI without a redeploy.

extension_points.name: STORAGE
extension_points.summary: Swap the object-storage backend (RecordingStorage implementation): any Django storage by default, bundled S3/MinIO backend, or your own.

extension_points.name: NORMALIZER
extension_points.summary: Swap the audio-normalization step: ffmpeg by default, a passthrough for environments without it, or a custom converter.

extension_points.name: SOURCE_TYPES
extension_points.summary: Extend the recording source-kind registry ({key: label}, merged over the built-in meet/dictaphone/upload/other) — add zoom, teams, phone and so on from settings without editing a code enum.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (recording.stage pipeline driving, llm.transcribe / llm.summarize calls), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry, AppSettings config layer
provides the llm.transcribe and llm.summarize comm Functions the transcribe/merge stages call; recordings passes audio and options by comm name, no import
every endpoint requires an authenticated user (IsAuthenticated); stapel-auth is the shelf's session issuer — any stapel-core-compatible JWT issuer satisfies the check

config_md: | PIPELINE | env | ordered stage-name list run by the generic driver (reorder/drop/insert stages). | no | [convert, transcribe, diarize, merge] |
| STAGES | env | overlay of custom/replacement stage handlers {name: dotted-path}; None removes a built-in. | no | {} |
| PIPELINE_RESOLVER | env | dotted path to `(recording) -> list[str]` — the runtime pipeline seam. | no | stapel_recordings.pipeline.default_pipeline_resolver |
| STORAGE | env | dotted path to a RecordingStorage implementation (REPLACE). | no | stapel_recordings.storage.DjangoStorageBackend |
| STORAGE_PREFIX | env | object-key prefix for stored recordings. | no | recordings |
| NORMALIZER | env | dotted path to `(src, dst) -> float|None` audio normalizer (REPLACE). | no | stapel_recordings.normalize.ffmpeg_normalize |
| SOURCE_TYPES | env | recording source-kind registry merged OVER the built-ins {key: label}. | no | {} |
| UPLOAD_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS | env | single-shot upload session lifetime. | no | 900 |
| MULTIPART_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS | env | multipart upload session lifetime. | no | 86400 |
| MULTIPART_PART_SIZE | env | multipart part size (bytes). | no | 10485760 |
| MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES | env | hard cap on an upload's total size. | no | 2147483648 |
| UPLOAD_EXTENSION_ALLOWLIST | env | accepted upload file extensions (lower-case, no dot). | no | mp3, m4a, wav, ogg, … (audio/video) |
| TRANSCRIBE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS | env | per-transcribe-stage timeout. | no | 1800 |
| MAX_STAGE_RETRIES | env | max retries per pipeline stage. | no | 3 |
| SUMMARIZE_ENABLED | env | whether the summarize step runs (delegated to stapel-agent). | no | true |
| SUMMARIZE_MODEL | env | agent model size used for summaries. | no | medium |
| STUCK_THRESHOLD_SECONDS | env | reconcile watchdog: age at which a running stage is re-emitted (must exceed the longest stage). | no | 2100 |
| ABANDONED_UPLOAD_THRESHOLD_SECONDS | env | age at which an incomplete upload session is abandoned. | no | 3600 |
| FFMPEG_BIN | env | ffmpeg binary path/name. | no | ffmpeg |
| FFPROBE_BIN | env | ffprobe binary path/name. | no | ffprobe |
| FFMPEG_TIMEOUT_S | env | subprocess timeout (seconds) for an ffmpeg/ffprobe call. | no | 1800 |

modules.provides: Target-generic reviews and ratings: an author rates and reviews an opaque host-defined target (target_type + target_key), driven by a per-target-type policy registry (who may review, pre/post moderation, one-review-per-author, owner responses) whose authority questions are answered by host comm callbacks. The module owns the per-target aggregate (avg/count over published reviews) and emits a generic visibility-change fact carrying it, so a host catalog maintains its own rating projection without calling back.

gates.behavior: post: reviews are published on creation and hidden reactively; pre: reviews are created pending and become visible only when a moderator publishes them.

curated.summary: The default moderation mode for target types that do not override it. "post" (default): a review is published the moment it is written and a moderator may hide it after the fact — the fast, open model. "pre": a review is held pending and stays invisible until a moderator publishes it — the curated model. A target type may override this per type. Values: post, pre.

gates.behavior: true: a target owner (as authorized by the type's can_moderate callback) may add one response per review; false: the response endpoint is rejected for the type.

curated.summary: Whether the target owner may attach a single public reply to a review by default. true (default): owner responses are enabled; false: reviews carry no owner reply. A target type may override this per type. Values: true, false.

extension_points.name: TARGET_TYPES
extension_points.summary: The flagship seam: the module ships knowing NO target types. A host registers what may be reviewed ({type_name: policy}, merged over the empty built-ins; None removes a type). A policy sets moderation (pre/post), one-review-per-author, whether owner responses are allowed, and — crucially — the comm Function names the module calls to ask the host 'may this author review?' (can_review) and 'may this actor moderate/respond?' (can_moderate), so the module never imports a host model.

extension_points.name: reviews.review.published
extension_points.summary: The projection hook: when a review becomes visible the module emits this fact carrying the freshly recomputed target aggregate {avg, count}; a host catalog subscribes to maintain its own avg_rating projection per (target_type, target_key) without calling back (§10 projection pattern).

extension_points.name: reviews.review.hidden
extension_points.summary: The counterpart fact: when a review leaves the visible set (moderated out) the module emits it with the updated aggregate, so the host projection stays consistent.

extension_points.name: reviews.aggregate
extension_points.summary: The module-owned aggregate (avg/count over published reviews) as a synchronous read primitive other services can call by name — the host projection is a cache of exactly this.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (review.published / review.hidden emits, reviews.aggregate Function, host policy callbacks), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry, AppSettings config layer
every endpoint requires an authenticated user (IsAuthenticated); stapel-auth is the shelf's session issuer — any stapel-core-compatible JWT issuer satisfies the check

config_md: | TARGET_TYPES | env | flagship seam: {type_name: policy} merged OVER the (empty) built-ins; None removes a type. Policies name comm-Function callbacks for eligibility/moderation. | no | {} |
| MODERATION_DEFAULT | env | axis: `post` (published on creation, hideable later) or `pre` (held pending until published). A type policy may override. | no | post |
| RESPONSES | env | axis: whether a target owner may attach a single Response to a review. A type policy may override. | no | true |
| RATING_MIN | env | inclusive minimum rating (out-of-range is a 400). | no | 1 |
| RATING_MAX | env | inclusive maximum rating (out-of-range is a 400). | no | 5 |

modules.provides: Video calls: rooms with shareable join codes, an access-level admission model (public / scope-trusted / restricted lobby) with a realtime waiting room over WebSockets, host admit/deny controls, join-token minting through a pluggable video-provider seam (LiveKit by default), and a recording-egress seam (start/stop + a video.egress_ended event) that integrates with stapel-recordings by event, never by import.

gates.behavior: A dotted path to a VideoProvider implementation; the default is stapel_video.providers.livekit.LiveKitProvider.

curated.summary: Selects the video-conferencing vendor behind every call. The default routes calls through LiveKit; point it at another backend that implements the provider seam to switch vendors without code changes. Changes who carries the media, not which endpoints exist.
curated.business_label: Which service runs the video calls

gates.behavior: public: anyone with the code auto-admits; scope_trusted: scope members auto-admit, others lobby; restricted: only the host auto-admits, everyone else lobbies.

curated.summary: The admission level a room gets when the creator does not pick one. "public": anyone with the join code joins instantly. "scope_trusted": members of the same workspace/org/tenant join instantly, outsiders wait. "restricted" (default): everyone but the host waits in the lobby. A client can always override per room.
curated.business_label: How open new rooms are by default

gates.behavior: true: non-auto-admitted joiners wait for host admission; false: the lobby is off and joiners are admitted directly.

curated.summary: Whether a freshly created room turns the lobby on by default (guests who are not auto-admitted wait for a host to let them in). True by default — the safe, gate-the-door setting; set False for open drop-in rooms. A client can override per room.
curated.business_label: Whether new rooms start with a waiting room

extension_points.name: VIDEO_PROVIDER
extension_points.summary: Swap the video backend (the VideoProvider ABC: mint join token, create room, start/stop recording egress, verify webhook). The default is the LiveKit implementation behind the [livekit] extra; point it at your own backend to change vendor without forking.

extension_points.name: SCOPE_PROVIDER
extension_points.summary: Swap how the opaque scope_key (workspace/org/tenant) is resolved from the request and how scope membership is decided — membership is what makes a scope_trusted room auto-admit the caller. The default is a single global scope where every authenticated user is a member.

extension_points.name: video.egress_ended
extension_points.summary: The recording seam: when a room recording finishes, the module emits this event carrying the storage key — stapel-recordings (or any subscriber) finalizes the upload. This library ships no recording pipeline and imports no recordings model.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

requires.reason: comm bus (video.egress_ended emit, user.deleted consume), JWT authentication (HTTP and Channels), AppSettings config layer, AnchorPagination
every endpoint but the provider webhook requires an authenticated user (IsAuthenticated); stapel-auth is the shelf's session issuer — any stapel-core-compatible JWT issuer satisfies the check
subscribes to video.egress_ended to finalize the recording upload the egress wrote; recording degrades to a no-op emit without a subscriber

config_md: | VIDEO_PROVIDER | env | axis: dotted path to a VideoProvider — which vendor carries the calls (SDK imported lazily). | no | stapel_video.providers.livekit.LiveKitProvider |
| SCOPE_PROVIDER | env | dotted path to a ScopeProvider (scope membership for the scope_trusted auto-admit). | no | stapel_video.scope.DefaultScopeProvider |
| DEFAULT_ACCESS_LEVEL | env | axis: default room access — public \| scope_trusted \| restricted. | no | restricted |
| DEFAULT_ADMIT_REQUIRED | env | axis: whether a fresh room starts with the lobby on. | no | true |
| LIVEKIT_URL | env | LiveKit server URL (default provider). | no | (empty) |
| LIVEKIT_API_KEY | vault | LiveKit API key (default provider). | no | (empty) |
| LIVEKIT_API_SECRET | vault | LiveKit API secret (default provider). | no | (empty) |
| JOIN_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS | env | join-token time-to-live (seconds). | no | 3600 |
| EGRESS_S3_ENDPOINT | env | recording-egress object-store endpoint (egress seam, off by default). | no | (empty) |
| EGRESS_S3_BUCKET | env | recording-egress bucket. | no | (empty) |
| EGRESS_S3_ACCESS_KEY | vault | recording-egress access key. | no | (empty) |
| EGRESS_S3_SECRET_KEY | vault | recording-egress secret key. | no | (empty) |

modules.provides: Team workspaces and tenancy: personal and work workspaces with owner/admin/member/viewer roles, email invitations with single-use tokens, last-owner protection, per-workspace settings and storage quotas, plus internal service-to-service membership checks — the foundational scope other modules attach their workspace_id to.

extension_points.name: Workspace.settings
extension_points.summary: A JSON bag on every workspace, PATCHable through the workspace API — the sanctioned place for app-level per-workspace preferences without schema changes.

extension_points.name: AUTH_USER_MODEL
extension_points.summary: Standard Django user swap — owner/member/inviter FKs target settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL; the module resolves users via get_user_model(), never a concrete class.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every public view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamsMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

extension_points.name: role_capabilities
extension_points.summary: Map the fixed owner/admin/member/viewer roles to app-specific capabilities in host code via role_at_least/require_role (in-service) or the workspaces.check_membership comm Function (cross-service, no import).

requires.reason: comm bus (workspace.* emits, workspaces.check_membership Function, user.registered consumer), JWT authentication, GDPR provider registry
emits the user.registered event that triggers personal-workspace creation and issues the authenticated sessions the workspace endpoints require; decoupled via the comm bus
delivers the workspace.invitation email this module requests via request_notification; invitation creation succeeds regardless (best-effort)
